claim: UCB T cells reduce TCR diversity after transplantation.

result.claim: UCB T cells reduce TCR diversity after transplantation.
result.reasoning: The claim states that UCB T cells reduce TCR diversity after transplantation. However, evidence from paper ID 27910499, titled "Quantitative assessment of T-cell repertoire recovery after hematopoietic stem cell transplantation," directly refutes this. Proposition 4 from this paper states, "After 6 months, cord blood-graft recipients approximated the TCR diversity of healthy individuals." The abstract of the same paper further clarifies that "cord blood-graft recipients approximated the TCR diversity of healthy individuals, whereas recipients of T cell-depleted peripheral-blood stem cell grafts had 28-fold and 14-fold lower CD4(+) and CD8(+) T cell diversities, respectively." This indicates that cord blood (UCB) grafts lead to a recovery of TCR diversity to healthy levels, rather than a reduction. In contrast, T cell-depleted peripheral-blood stem cell grafts were associated with reduced TCR diversity.

evidence.text: Delayed T cell recovery and restricted T cell receptor diversity after allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation are associated with increased risks of infection and cancer relapse.
evidence.source: Quantitative assessment of T-cell repertoire recovery after hematopoietic stem cell transplantation

evidence.text: Golli-deficient T cells showed enhanced calcium entry upon T cell receptor stimulation.
evidence.source: Golli protein negatively regulates store depletion-induced calcium influx in T cells.

evidence.text: After 12 months, the deficiencies in CD8(+) T cell diversity had not improved.
evidence.source: Quantitative assessment of T-cell repertoire recovery after hematopoietic stem cell transplantation

evidence.text: T-zone chemokines guide activated B cells to the T zone.
evidence.source: Finding the right niche: B-cell migration in the early phases of T-dependent antibody responses.

evidence.text: Effector memory T cells (T(EM)) were found only in the lungs and small intestine of pediatric subjects.

evidence.text: Regulatory T (T(reg)) cells comprise 30-40% of CD4(+) T cells in pediatric tissues.

evidence.text: In adult tissues the main subset of T cells are memory T cells.

evidence.text: T cell receptor (TCR-CD3) triggering involves both receptor clustering and conformational changes at the cytoplasmic tails of the CD3 subunits.
evidence.source: Full activation of the T cell receptor requires both clustering and conformational changes at CD3.

evidence.text: Memory CD4+ T cell–induced IICs and viral control are optimal when memory cells are either T helper type 1 (TH1) or TH17 polarized.
evidence.source: Memory CD4+ T cells induce innate responses independently of pathogen

evidence.text: T cell activation is predicated on the interaction between the T cell receptor and peptide-major histocompatibility ligands.
evidence.source: The stimulatory potency of T cell antigens is influenced by the formation of the immunological synapse.

evidence.text: Treg cells lacking expression of integrin αvβ8 were unable to suppress pathogenic T cell responses during active inflammation.
